title: rubus amoenus subsp. praedatus
intro: Rubus amoenus subsp. praedatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of shrub native to North America. The thimbleberry is a member of the rose family and is recognized by its edible, sweet-tasting berries and its uses in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of the thimbleberry include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The thimbleberry can be prepared as herbal teas, salves, or infusions, and has been used to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health.
